title: Smoke-Hot Dense Filipino Puy-Lentils Stew
description: A briny, dense hangover-cure Filipino dish featuring puy-lentils and a smoke-hot flavor profile, suitable for those who can't handle onions or garlic.
instructions: Place the lentils in a large pot and cover with 4 cups of water.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce heat to a simmer and cook the lentils for 20 minutes.
Meanwhile, in a separate pan, cook the pork belly over medium heat until crispy, then chop into small pieces.
Add the cooked pork belly, fish sauce, black peppercorns, coriander, smoked paprika, and salt to the pot with the lentils.
Stir to combine and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Add the water and chili flakes to the pot and stir to combine.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce heat to a simmer and cook for 30 minutes, or until the lentils are tender.
Garnish with chopped cilantro before serving.
Serve hot, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
